verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type-I transmembrane protein (single-pass, ectodomain + TM region) that carries its own membrane anchor. However, its expression is strictly restricted to spermatozoa (germline/gamete lineage). Within that lineage it resides in the inner acrosomal membrane of non-capacitated sperm and translocates to the outer plasma membrane surface during/after the acrosome reaction, where it engages oocyte JUNO to mediate gamete fusion. Surface display is real and TM-anchored, but confined to a narrow germline/reproductive cell lineage — qualifying as contextual / tissue_restricted_surface rather than a broadly surface-accessible protein.
